title: Climate Change and Predictability Coursework

prerequisites.text: A relevant Honours degree (or equivalent). Students with backgrounds in scientific, planning, engineering, economic, educational, social and legal disciplines are encouraged to apply.

outline: This full-time taught Masters course (MSc or MPhil) is offered over 13 months beginning in January. It provides interdisciplinary training in climate change and sustainable development, with a focus on issues relevant to African development. The curriculum comprises two compulsory core courses — EGS5031F (Introduction to Climate Change & Sustainable Development) and EGS5032F/S (Climate Change Adaptation & Mitigation) — plus at least two elective courses from a range of modules allowing students to explore new areas or engage climate and development through existing disciplinary backgrounds.
assessment: Students must pass all coursework with a sub-minimum of 33% for each core or elective course module; an aggregate coursework mark of 50% is required. The minor dissertation component counts 50% of the degree.
